title: Final Capstone: Inventory Operations Launch
summary: Build a two-table makers-market inventory database through six cumulative schema and data-management steps.

sketchBlocks.title: Inventory Operations Launch Project
sketchBlocks.bodyMarkdown: In this final capstone project, you will take on the role of an SQL data management specialist for a neighborhood makers market. The market is transitioning from a shared spreadsheet to a small operational database. Your task is to create a launch script that will define the database schema, load approved product and stock event data, apply necessary corrections, and finalize the database state for verification by another analyst. This project will demonstrate your ability to manage data integrity, apply precise updates, and ensure the database is ready for operational use.

quizDraft.title: Build the Product Table
quizDraft.prompt: Create the `products` table with the specified columns and constraints in `schema.sql`.

solutionFiles.content:
CREATE TABLE products (
    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
    sku TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,
    name TEXT NOT NULL,
    category TEXT NOT NULL,
    price REAL NOT NULL,
    status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'draft',
    notes TEXT NULL
);

quizDraft.solutionCode:
CREATE TABLE products (
    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
    sku TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,
    name TEXT NOT NULL,
    category TEXT NOT NULL,
    price REAL NOT NULL,
    status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'draft',
    notes TEXT NULL
);
quizDraft.checkSql: SELECT name FROM sqlite_master WHERE type='table' AND name='products';
quizDraft.hint: Define each column with its data type and constraints.

help.concept: Creating a table with specific columns and constraints ensures data integrity.
help.hint_2: Ensure `sku` is unique and `status` has a default value.

quizDraft.title: Build the Stock Event Table
quizDraft.prompt: Create the `stock_events` table with the specified columns and constraints in `schema.sql`.

solutionFiles.content:
CREATE TABLE products (
    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
    sku TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,
    name TEXT NOT NULL,
    category TEXT NOT NULL,
    price REAL NOT NULL,
    status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'draft',
    notes TEXT NULL
);

CREATE TABLE stock_events (
    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
    product_id INTEGER NOT NULL,
    quantity_change INTEGER NOT NULL,
    event_type TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'receipt',
    note TEXT NULL,
    FOREIGN KEY (product_id) REFERENCES products(id)
);

quizDraft.solutionCode:
CREATE TABLE products (
    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
    sku TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,
    name TEXT NOT NULL,
    category TEXT NOT NULL,
    price REAL NOT NULL,
    status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'draft',
    notes TEXT NULL
);

CREATE TABLE stock_events (
    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
    product_id INTEGER NOT NULL,
    quantity_change INTEGER NOT NULL,
    event_type TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'receipt',
    note TEXT NULL,
    FOREIGN KEY (product_id) REFERENCES products(id)
);
quizDraft.checkSql: SELECT name FROM sqlite_master WHERE type='table' AND name='stock_events';
quizDraft.hint: Include a foreign key constraint to link to the `products` table.

help.concept: Creating a table with a foreign key ensures relational integrity between tables.
help.hint_1: Use `FOREIGN KEY` to reference the `products` table.

solutionFiles.content:
CREATE TABLE products (
    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
    sku TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,
    name TEXT NOT NULL,
    category TEXT NOT NULL,
    price REAL NOT NULL,
    status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'draft',
    notes TEXT NULL
);

CREATE TABLE stock_events (
    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
    product_id INTEGER NOT NULL,
    quantity_change INTEGER NOT NULL,
    event_type TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'receipt',
    note TEXT NULL,
    FOREIGN KEY (product_id) REFERENCES products(id)
);

INSERT INTO products (sku, name, category, price, notes) VALUES
    ('SKU001', 'Product A', 'Category 1', 19.99, NULL),
    ('SKU002', 'Product B', 'Category 2', 29.99, 'Special edition'),
    ('SKU003', 'Product C', 'Category 3', 39.99, NULL);

quizDraft.solutionCode:
INSERT INTO products (sku, name, category, price, notes) VALUES
    ('SKU001', 'Product A', 'Category 1', 19.99, NULL),
    ('SKU002', 'Product B', 'Category 2', 29.99, 'Special edition'),
    ('SKU003', 'Product C', 'Category 3', 39.99, NULL);
quizDraft.hint: Use explicit column lists for each INSERT statement.

help.concept: Inserting data with explicit columns ensures clarity and control over defaults.
help.hint_1: Include all necessary columns in the INSERT statement.
help.hint_2: Omit `status` in one INSERT to use the default value.

    status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'draft',
    notes TEXT NULL
);

CREATE TABLE stock_events (
    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
    product_id INTEGER NOT NULL,
    quantity_change INTEGER NOT NULL,
    event_type TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'receipt',
    note TEXT NULL,
    FOREIGN KEY (product_id) REFERENCES products(id)
);

INSERT INTO products (sku, name, category, price, notes) VALUES
    ('SKU001', 'Product A', 'Category 1', 19.99, NULL),
    ('SKU002', 'Product B', 'Category 2', 29.99, 'Special edition'),
    ('SKU003', 'Product C', 'Category 3', 39.99, NULL);

INSERT INTO stock_events (product_id, quantity_change, event_type, note) VALUES

    price = 24.99,
    status = 'active'
WHERE

quizDraft.solutionCode:
UPDATE products
SET

    status = 'active'

    id = 1;
quizDraft.checkSql: SELECT price, status FROM products WHERE id = 1;
quizDraft.hint: Use a WHERE clause to target the specific product by ID.

help.concept: Updating specific rows requires a precise WHERE clause to avoid unintended changes.
help.hint_2: Ensure the WHERE clause targets the correct product ID.

quizDraft.title: Remove the Test Event and Finalize
quizDraft.prompt: Delete the test event from `stock_events` in `query.sql` using a narrow WHERE clause.
quizDraft.starterCode:
UPDATE products
SET
    price = 24.99,
    status = 'active'
WHERE
    id = 1;

-- Delete the test event
DELETE FROM stock_events
WHERE
    -- Specify the test event condition
;

quizDraft.hint: Identify the test event by its unique note or ID.

help.hint_1: Use `DELETE FROM` with a WHERE clause.
